evals.name: admin-deletes-user-persists
evals.prompt: I need to add an e2e scenario for user-management: an admin deletes a user, then reloads the page, and the user must still be gone. We have a backend, just tell me how to set this up in this repo.
evals.expected_output: Recommends §4.3 API seeding: new seeder under src/support/user-management/seeders/, uses APIRequestContext, names entity e2e-<runId>-*, tracks IDs on CustomWorld.seededEntities, cleans up in After hook. Does NOT recommend mocking, because the assertion is real persistence across reload.

evals.name: empty-state-mock
evals.prompt: Can you add a cucumber scenario that shows the empty state message on the user list page when the API returns zero records? I don't want to touch the real backend for this.
evals.expected_output: Recommends §4.4 mock: extend GraphQLMockBase with a user-management mock, payload loaded from fixture (e.g. list-empty.json), registered in a Before hook or Given step. Points to existing src/support/user-management/mocks/user-management.mock.ts and src/support/user-management/fixtures/users/list-empty.json.

evals.name: pagination-25-unique-users
evals.prompt: I want a test that verifies the user list pagination works with 25 users. Each user should have a different name and email. No need to actually create them in the backend.
evals.expected_output: Recommends §4.2 builder+faker to generate 25 unique users (uniqueSuffix / buildUniqueEmail, global FAKER_SEED for determinism), combined with §4.4 mock to return those generated users as the API response. Does NOT recommend seeding (no backend persistence needed) and does NOT recommend a static fixture (varied data).

evals.name: login-locked-account
evals.prompt: Add a login scenario for a locked-out account that sees the 'Your account is locked' error message.
evals.expected_output: Recommends §4.1 static fixture: reuse or extend src/support/authentication/fixtures/login/users.json with a lockedUser entry, load via FixtureLoader.loadUserCredentials('lockedUser'). Credentials are stable, committed, reference-like.
